explanation.summary: Vitamins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 48 from a base nutrition score of 48. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: brings a clean fruit identity
topPros.explanation: It still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

topPros.title: easy to use as a whole food
topPros.explanation: Simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating.

topPros.title: usually beats ultra-processed sweet snacks
topPros.explanation: That replacement value matters in real life.

topCons.title: can be easy to waste if timing is off
topCons.explanation: Perishability is a practical downside people actually feel.

topCons.title: protein contribution is tiny
topCons.explanation: That is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth.

topCons.title: satiety is not especially strong
topCons.explanation: Fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Kiwi has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
